name: fideslang

metadata.name: fideslang
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation:
# Fideslang

[![License: CC BY 4.0](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-CC%20BY%204.0-lightgrey.svg)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/) [![Twitter](https://img.shields.io/twitter/follow/ethyca?style=social)](https://twitter.com/ethyca)

![Fideslang banner](mkdocs/docs/img/fideslang.png "Fideslang banner")

## Overview

Fideslang or Fides Language is a privacy taxonomy and working draft of a proposed structure to describe data and data processing behaviors as part of a typical software development process. Our hope with standardizing this definition publicly with the community is to derive an interoperable standard for describing types of data and how they're being used in applications to simplify global privacy regulations.

**To view the detailed taxonomy documentation, please visit [https://ethyca.github.io/fideslang/](https://ethyca.github.io/fideslang)**

## Summary of Taxonomy Classification Groups

The taxonomy is currently comprised of three classification groups that are used together to easily describe the data types and associated processing behaviors of an entire tech stack; both the application processes and any data storage.

[Click here to view an interactive visualization of the taxonomy](https://ethyca.github.io/fideslang/explorer/)

### 1. Data Categories

Data Categories are labels used to describe the type of data processed by a system. You can assign one or more data categories to a field when classifying a system.

Data Categories are hierarchical with natural inheritance, meaning you can classify data coarsely with a high-level category (e.g. `user.contact` data), or you can classify it with greater precision using subclasses (e.g. `user.contact.email` data).

Learn more about [Data Categories in the taxonomy reference now](https://ethyca.github.io/fideslang/taxonomy/data_categories/).

### 2. Data Use Categories

Data Use Categories are labels that describe how, or for what purpose(s) a component of your system is using data. Similar to data categories, you can assign one or multiple Data Use Categories to a system.

Data Use Categories are also hierarchical with natural inheritance, meaning you can easily describe what you're using data for either coarsely (e.g. `provide.service.operations`) or with more precision using subclasses (e.g. `provide.service.operations.support.optimization`).

Learn more about [Data Use Categories in the taxonomy reference now](https://ethyca.github.io/fideslang/data_uses/).

### 3. Data Subject Categories

"Data Subject" is a label commonly used in the regulatory world to describe the users of a system whose data is being processed. In many systems a generic user label may be sufficient, however the Privacy Taxonomy is intended to provide greater control through specificity where needed.

Examples of a Data Subject are:

- `anonymous_user`
- `employee`
- `customer`
- `patient`
- `next_of_kin`

Learn more about [Data Subject Categories in the taxonomy reference now](https://ethyca.github.io/fideslang/taxonomy/data_subjects/).

### Extensibility & Interoperability

The taxonomy is designed to support common privacy compliance regulations and standards out of the box, these include GDPR, CCPA, LGPD and ISO 19944.

You can extend the taxonomy to support your system needs. If you do this, we recommend extending from the existing class structures to ensure interoperability inside and outside your organization.

If you have suggestions for missing classifications or concepts, please submit them for addition.

classes.name: fideslang.gvl.models.Purpose
documentation: Pydantic model for GVL purpose records

classes.name: fideslang.gvl.models.MappedPurpose
documentation:
Extension of the base GVL purpose model to include properties related to fideslang mappings.

This is separated from the base GVL purpose model to keep that model a "pristine" representation
of GVL source data.

classes.name: fideslang.gvl.models.Feature
documentation: Pydantic model for GVL feature records

classes.name: fideslang.gvl.models.GVLDataCategory
documentation: Pydantic model for GVL data category records

classes.name: fideslang.gvl.models.MappedDataCategory
documentation:
Extension of the base GVL data category model to include properties related to fideslang mappings.

This is separated from the base GVL data category model to keep that model a "pristine" representation
of GVL source data.

modules.name: fideslang.gvl
documentation: pylint: disable=too-many-locals

functions.name: fideslang.gvl.purpose_to_data_use
documentation:
Utility function to return the fideslang data uses associated with the
given GVL purpose (or special purpose) ID.

By default, the given ID is treated as a purpose ID. The `special_purpose`
argument can be set to `True` if looking up special purpose IDs.

Raises a KeyError if an invalid purpose ID is provided.

functions.name: fideslang.gvl.data_use_to_purpose
documentation:
Utility function to return the GVL purpose (or special purpose) associated
with the given fideslang data use.

Returns None if no associated purpose (or special purpose) is found

functions.name: fideslang.gvl.feature_name_to_feature
documentation: Utility function to return a GVL feature (or special feature) given the feature's name

functions.name: fideslang.gvl.feature_id_to_feature_name
documentation: Utility function to return a GVL feature/special feature name given the feature/special feature's id

functions.name: fideslang.gvl.data_category_id_to_data_categories
documentation:
Utility function to return the fideslang data categories associated with the
given GVL data category ID.

Raises a KeyError if an invalid GVL data category ID is provided.

modules.name: fideslang.utils
documentation: Utils for use within various fideslang modules.

functions.name: fideslang.utils.get_resource_by_fides_key
documentation: Recurse through a taxonomy to find a specific resource its fides_key.

modules.name: fideslang.parse
documentation:
This module handles everything related to parsing resources into Pydantic models,
either from local files or the server.

functions.name: fideslang.parse.parse_dict
documentation: Parse an individual resource into its Python model.

functions.name: fideslang.parse.load_manifests_into_taxonomy
documentation: Parse the raw resource manifests into resource resources.

modules.name: fideslang
documentation: Exports various fideslang objects for easier use elsewhere.

modules.name: fideslang.relationships
documentation:
This module is responsible for calculating what resources are referenced
by each other and building a dependency graph of relationships.

functions.name: fideslang.relationships.find_nested_keys_in_list
documentation: Iterates a nested object list and returns any keys nested fides keys

functions.name: fideslang.relationships.find_referenced_fides_keys
documentation:
Use type-signature introspection to figure out which fields
include the FidesKey type and return all of those values.

Note that this finds _all_ fides_keys, including the resource's own fides_key

This function is used recursively for arbitrary-depth objects.

functions.name: fideslang.relationships.get_referenced_missing_keys
documentation:
Iterate through the Taxonomy and create a set of all of the FidesKeys
that are contained within it.

modules.name: fideslang._version
documentation: file generated by setuptools_scm
don't change, don't track in version control

modules.name: fideslang.validation
documentation: Contains all of the additional validation for the resource models.

functions.name: fideslang.validation.validate_fides_key
documentation: Throws ValueError if val is not a valid FidesKey

functions.name: fideslang.validation.sort_list_objects_by_name
documentation:
Sort objects in a list by their name.
This makes resource comparisons deterministic.

functions.name: fideslang.validation.unique_items_in_list
documentation:
Verify that the `name` attributes of each item in the provided list are unique.

This is useful for fields where there is no FidesKey but we want to
do a uniqueness check.

functions.name: fideslang.validation.no_self_reference
documentation:
Check to make sure that the fides_key doesn't match other fides_key
references within an object.

i.e. DataCategory.parent_key != DataCategory.fides_key

functions.name: fideslang.validation.deprecated_version_later_than_added
documentation:
Check to make sure that the deprecated version is later than the added version.

This will also catch errors where the deprecated version is defined but the added
version is empty.

functions.name: fideslang.validation.has_versioning_if_default
documentation: Check to make sure that version fields are set for default items.

functions.name: fideslang.validation.is_deprecated_if_replaced
documentation: Check to make sure that the item has been deprecated if there is a replacement.

functions.name: fideslang.validation.matching_parent_key
documentation: Confirm that the parent_key matches the parent parsed from the FidesKey.

functions.name: fideslang.validation.parse_data_type_string
documentation:
Parse the data type string. Arrays are expressed in the form 'type[]'.

e.g.
- 'string' -> ('string', false)
- 'string[]' -> ('string', true)

These data_types are for use in DatasetField.fides_meta.

functions.name: fideslang.validation.is_valid_data_type
documentation: Is this type a valid data type identifier in fides?

functions.name: fideslang.validation.valid_data_type
documentation: If the data_type is provided ensure that it is a member of DataType.

functions.name: fideslang.validation.validate_path_of_url
documentation: Converts an AnyUrl to a string

functions.name: fideslang.validation.validate_path_of_http_url
documentation: Converts an AnyHttpUrl to a string

classes.name: fideslang.validation.FidesValidationError
documentation: Custom exception for when the pydantic ValidationError can't be used.

modules.name: fideslang.manifests
documentation: This module handles anything related to working with raw manifest files.

functions.name: fideslang.manifests.write_manifest
documentation: Write a dict representation of a resource out to a file.

functions.name: fideslang.manifests.load_yaml_into_dict
documentation: This loads yaml files into a dictionary to be used in API calls.

functions.name: fideslang.manifests.union_manifests
documentation:
Combine all of the manifests into a single dictionary,
appending resource values with the same keys.

functions.name: fideslang.manifests.ingest_manifests
documentation:
Ingest either a single file or all of the manifests available in a
directory and concatenate them into a single object.

Directories will be searched recursively.

Generate default taxonomy objects.

Given that we know these are defaults, set default values accordingly.

modules.name: fideslang.default_fixtures
documentation:
This is a slowly changing dataset built from a public endpoint
and stored here as a constant to reduce non-value added api calls.

https://restcountries.com/v2/all?fields=name,alpha3Code

To update:
1. Make a GET request to the above url
2. Copy/Paste the JSON response below

The model defining the responsibility or role over
the system that processes personal data.

Used to identify whether the organization is a
Controller, Processor, or Sub-Processor of the data

Determine whether or not defined rights are
being included or excluded.

classes.name: fideslang.models.DataSubjectRightsEnum

The model for data subject rights over
personal data.

Based upon chapter 3 of the GDPR

classes.name: fideslang.models.LegalBasisForProcessingEnum

The model for allowable legal basis categories on privacy declarations.

Based upon article 6 of the GDPR

classes.name: fideslang.models.LegalBasisForProfilingEnum
documentation: The model for describing the legal basis under which profiling is performed

classes.name: fideslang.models.LegalBasisForTransfersEnum
documentation:
The model for describing the legal basis under which data is transferred

We currently do _not_ enforce this enum on the `legal_basis_for_transfers`
field, because the set of allowable values seems to be changing frequently
and without clear notice in upstream, public data sources.

classes.name: fideslang.models.SpecialCategoryLegalBasisEnum
documentation:
The model for the legal basis for processing special categories of personal data
on privacy declarations

Based upon article 9 of the GDPR

classes.name: fideslang.models.DatasetFieldBase
documentation:
Base DatasetField Resource model.

This model is available for cases where the DatasetField information needs to be
customized. In general this will not be the case and you will instead want to use
the DatasetField model.

When this model is used you will need to implement your own recursive field in
to adding any new needed fields.

Example:

```py
from typing import List, Optional
from fideslang import DatasetFieldBase

class MyDatasetField(DatasetFieldBase):
    custom: str
    fields: Optional[List[MyDatasetField]] = []
```
Dataset

methods.name: fideslang.models.DataFlow.user_special_case
documentation:
If either the `fides_key` or the `type` are set to "user",
then the other must also be set to "user".

methods.name: fideslang.models.DataFlow.verify_type_is_flowable
documentation:
Assert that the value of the `type` field is a member

The DataFlow resource model.

Describes a resource model with which a given System resource communicates.

classes.name: fideslang.models.System

methods.name: fideslang.models.System.privacy_declarations_reference_data_flows
documentation:
Any `PrivacyDeclaration`s which include `egress` and/or `ingress` fields must
only reference the `fides_key`s of defined `DataFlow`s in said field(s).

The System resource model.

Describes an application and includes a list of PrivacyDeclaration resources.

classes.name: fideslang.models.Taxonomy
documentation:
Represents an entire taxonomy of Fides Resources.

The choice to not use pluralized forms of each resource name
was deliberate, as this would have caused huge amounts of complexity
elsewhere across the codebase.
Taxonomy
